frameworks.name: transcranial direct current stimulation neuromodulation
frameworks.one_line: anodal TDCS over M1-HAND probed for lasting shifts in corticospinal excitability

frameworks.name: individual-differences multimodal mapping
frameworks.one_line: linking per-subject MEP change to task-related fMRI activity in motor network

frameworks.name: motor evoked potential excitability assay
frameworks.one_line: single-pulse TMS-evoked MEPs index corticospinal output before and after TDCS

core_question: Does the individual change in corticospinal excitability after M1-HAND TDCS relate to changes in task-related motor cortical activity?
key_assumptions: anodal TDCS can shift corticospinal excitability beyond stimulation
task-related fMRI activity reflects cortical motor engagement
inter-individual variability is informative rather than noise
manipulations: real vs sham anodal TDCS at 0.75 mA for 20 min
key_findings: no consistent group-level aftereffect of real TDCS on mean MEP amplitude, task activity, or performance
individual MEP-amplitude change correlated positively with task-related activity change in supplementary motor area
individual MEP-amplitude change related to AP-LM MEP latency change
interpretations: TDCS aftereffects are best captured at the individual level rather than group means
corticospinal excitability shifts co-vary with supplementary motor area engagement
limitations_noted: small sample of seventeen healthy volunteers
no consistent mean effect of real TDCS
